text.title: Jira Workflow Management Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules grounding Jira Workflow Management. Each pairs a binary check and a sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Workflow definitions MUST live as version-controlled YAML in the project repo; UI-only edits forbidden.
rule.rationale: UI-only workflow edits drift between projects and break on cloud migrations; versioned YAML is reviewable and re-applicable.

rule.statement: Every workflow transition MUST declare its conditions (role / status / custom-field) and validators. Implicit 'open' transitions forbidden.
rule.rationale: Implicit transitions allow anyone to change status; conditions enforce business rules at the workflow boundary.

rule.statement: Recurring queries MUST be saved as named JQL filters, not pasted into search each time.
rule.rationale: Ad-hoc JQL drifts: contributors recreate near-duplicates with subtle bugs. Saved filters become a documented inventory.

rule.statement: Jira automation rules MUST declare rate limits and rule-execution scope (project / global) to prevent recursion loops.
rule.rationale: Unscoped automation has caused mass-update incidents (10k issues in 10 minutes); throttling contains blast radius.

rule.statement: API tokens used by agents MUST scope to read:jira-work + write:jira-work minimum. Admin tokens forbidden in automation.
rule.rationale: Admin tokens expose project administration; minimum-scope tokens contain blast radius on leak.

rule.statement: If preconditions for Jira Workflow Management fail (see Skip-If list in AGENTS.md), the operator MUST skip this methodology and document the fallback reason instead of fabricating an output.
rule.rationale: Applying Jira Workflow Management outside its envelope produces false-precision artefacts. Explicit skip preserves epistemic discipline.
